reason: Fixes meta-260606T0155Z-loop-surface-inject-spawnandcall-chicken-egg. The old code wrote the JSON-RPC initialize message inside the child.stdout.on("data") handler; since the first stdout data from a freshly spawned MCP server is the response to initialize, the send never fired and every session-start probe hit the 10s timeout. Phase 4 of plan 260605 then logged a meta_state_report finding (subtype=mcp-connection) and printed a banner — masking the underlying bug as a transient connection issue. Verified empirically 2026-06-06: before fix, hook output was the failure banner with reason=timeout; after fix, hook output is the success block (49 tools, 8 record types, 6 active findings). The new real-spawn test (loop-surface-inject-real-spawn.test.cjs) locks the end-to-end behavior by racing the probe against a 9s wall clock — the old code would have failed this test at ~9s. Closes Gap #2 (meta-260605T1356Z-sp0-sp3-tools-require-live-mcp-server-connection) empirically: the SessionStart probe now succeeds in real sessions, so the 5 SP0-SP3 tools are reachable via the canonical mcp__learning_loop_mcp__* surface.

reason: Closes the splitSegments-quote-unaware bug (finding meta-260606T0301Z-...). The P1 G8 subcommand-class fix (meta-260606T0225Z-...) shipped a refined rule pattern but assumed splitSegments handled quoted message bodies correctly. Empirically 2026-06-06, a multi-paragraph git commit describing the P1 fix itself (with trigger phrases like 'propose/design/create a new schema, new schema, create schema' inside -m bodies) was still escalated by the rule, because the ';' in 'false positives; CLI subcommand' split the body into 16 segments, and each fragment was checked independently by the regex. The new state machine keeps the entire quoted body as one segment, so stripMessageFlags correctly strips the -m value and the regex sees only 'git commit'. Blast radius: every applyPromotedRules + matchConstraintPattern caller. POSIX shell semantics preserved (single quotes are literal; double quotes allow backslash escapes; backslash outside quotes escapes the next char).

description:
Context pollution: stale workaround language + audit-trail noise bias fresh readers toward "G8 is still broken." The G8 subcommand-class + message-body-fragmentation classes are BOTH fixed (P1 refined regex at meta-260606T0225Z + P2 quote-aware splitSegments at the change-log resolving meta-260606T0301Z, status=resolved). Empirical probe 2026-06-06 confirms: (a) `git commit -m "propose/design/create a new schema pattern"` returns decision=ok (P2); (b) `ck plan create --title foo --phases bar` returns decision=ok (P1); (c) legitimate triggers like `echo propose/design/create a new schema` still escalate (rule still enforcing correctly).

Three pollution sources:

1. AGENTS.md "use Create tool directly" + "git commit -F /tmp/msg.txt" workarounds predate both P1 and P2. They are now stale (proven by this probe) but still steer fresh agents away from canonical paths. A fresh agent reading AGENTS.md will use -F for safety, masking any future P1/P2 regression instead of catching it. Mitigation: replace the workaround paragraphs with a "verified working" note pointing at the regression probes.

2. Meta-state.jsonl audit trail: 4 superseded G8 finding entries (recurrences 1, 3, 4, 5), 3 change-logs, the meta-260606T0301Z finding, and the active rule entry combine into ~9 entries that all narrate the G8 fix story. The entries are correctly linked (consolidated_into, supersedes), but a fresh reader skimming meta_state_list will see a wall of "G8" and bias toward "G8 is open." Mitigation: out of scope for this finding — the audit trail is by design.

3. Self-applied workaround during mcp fix: I used `git commit -F /tmp/commit-msg-N.txt` for commits 1692b84 and a35074a. The probe shows this was unnecessary — inline `-m "..."` with trigger phrases would have passed. Applying the old workaround to a fixed bug is itself the pollution: future agents following my commit history will copy the -F pattern without questioning it. Mitigation: the next commit can use inline -m to reset the example, but no retroactive fix is needed.

Recommended follow-up (separate work):
- Update AGENTS.md § "G8 Subcommand Class" + "Create tool fallback" sections to mark them as historical workarounds superseded by P1+P2.
- Add a permanent regression probe in `tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/` that runs the 5-case G8 matrix on every change to `core/gate-logic.js` or `promoted_to_rule.pattern` (locking in the fix so future regressions are caught).

title: Meta-state cross-reference fields — typed fields on rule and loop-design schemas
description:
Schema enhancement design captured for a future plan: add formal cross-reference field for this change-log/finding targets or relates to that finding relationships. Current state (2026-06-06): meta-state.jsonl has no formal related-to or design-target field. The only formal cross-reference fields are (1) consolidated_into (finding -> change-log; valid only with status=superseded), (2) consolidates (change-log -> finding; means these findings are superseded by this change-log), and (3) supersedes (change-log -> change-log). None of these express this design note targets that active finding. Proposed shape (option A): extend change-log applies_to to include findings: applies_to.findings: z.array(z.string()).optional(). Proposed shape (option B): add a generic related_to: z.array(z.string()).optional() to both finding and change-log schemas. Use case: design notes (like the cold-session test rule design captured in meta-260606T1531Z-cold-session-test-rule-deferred, which targets meta-260606T0443Z-mcp-tools-not-loaded-into-agent-tool-list) need to formally reference the target finding they are a design for. Workaround in use today: mention the target finding id in the description field (discoverable via grep, not via schema). Status: design note; deferred for a future plan per operator decision 2026-06-06. Operators and future plans discover this design note via meta_state_list with subtype=meta-state-schema-enhancement.

GAP ADDED 2026-06-08: The original design proposed typed cross-reference fields (proposed_design_for + addresses), but did not include the CRUD operations needed to maintain those fields. The MCP tool surface covers Create, Read, and (partially) Resolve, but lacks Update/Patch on existing entries. Concrete instance: this entry was just updated to add addresses=[the CRUD-coverage finding] via direct I/O (node -e importing core/meta-state.js#updateEntry) because meta_state_propose_design returns already_exists on id collision. The gap recurs in any context where an agent needs to update an existing entry: backfilling code_fingerprint on a resolved finding, updating evidence_code_ref after a :line -> #anchor refactor, refining a rule refinement_reason, etc. The fix must ship as part of this design: meta_state_patch (new tool with CAS) OR meta_state_propose_design update-or-create mode. Once the CRUD operation exists, the cross-reference fields become operationally useful (an agent can update proposed_design_for when the design evolves, can update addresses when a new finding motivates the design) rather than declarative-only (set at creation, frozen).

title: Meta-state registry → SQLite migration (trajectory; parked)
description: Trajectory design captured for a future plan: migrate the meta-state.jsonl registry to a SQLite database with the same MCP surface. Pre-conditions: registry > 2x current size (currently ~540KB JSONL with 500+ entries; 2x is ~1MB). Current Approach A (sidecar + LRU + L2 cache + batch + archive) covers the 3 active size-overrun findings structurally and gets 90% of the benefit at 20% of the touch surface. SQLite migration is parked per the anti-rationalization in plan 260608-2255: migration risk on 490+ entries + 30+ call sites + WSL2 build cost. When the pre-conditions are met, the migration will: (1) preserve the 4-kind union schema (finding | change-log | rule | loop-design), (2) preserve the per-root write queue, (3) port metaStateBatch to a single SQL transaction, (4) replace the LRU cache with SQLite query planner, (5) keep the sidecar cache for cold/compact tier payloads.

description: The 2026-06-09 MCP Server Stale-Code Problem session handoff mis-described the path-resolution fix in `meta_state_refresh_tools` as joining against `root`, producing paths like `<root>/tools/gate-tool.js`. The actual fix (confirmed by direct probe in the next session, then by end-to-end MCP smoke test) is `SERVER_DIR = join(__dirname, "..")` — i.e., paths are relative to `server.js` (which lives at `<server>/`), not the project root. The first attempted fix (described in the prior session's narrative) was wrong: Node ESM resolves the manifest paths relative to `server.js`'s directory, so `<server>/tools/gate-tool.js` is correct. The handoff also conflated two distinct guesses (the original double-`tools` bug, and the `root + file` "fix" that also failed). The corrected fix is in `tools/learning-loop-mcp/tools/meta-state-refresh-tools-tool.js:13-19` and the explanatory JSDoc is at lines 21-32. The end-to-end smoke test on 2026-06-09 confirmed `refreshed_count=55, status=refreshed` over the MCP surface. Subtype: `handoff-error`. Subtype taxonomy: session-narrative entries that mis-describe shipped fixes. The followup is needed so that future agents reading prior session narratives do not re-implement the wrong fix when re-deriving the path semantics, and so that the loop's self-model has an accurate record of how the bug was actually fixed.

reason: Links meta-260606T1500Z-closeout-script-idempotency-bug to meta-260608T0847Z-ttl-expire-system-has-the-wrong-action-for-expiry-current-se as a concrete case study of the TTL system's broken semantics. The TTL finding predicted that auto-resolve on expiry would make bugs disappear without trace; the closeout finding is the proof. This session (2026-06-09) only found the closeout bug because the operator explicitly asked to check it — the active set had already lost it to auto-resolve. The TTL finding itself shares the same fate: it was auto-resolved at 2026-06-09T02:10:37Z despite describing a still-active design flaw. Core insight: expired != resolved. A finding may be expired but the bug may still exist, or conversely the bug may be fixed but the finding was never properly resolved. The registry needs a stale-flag model (not auto-resolve) plus a meta_state_re_verify tool, per the TTL finding's own redesign proposal.

title: meta_state_patch wire-format recursion: unwrap {item:[...]} + ZodObject passthrough recursion
description: The meta_state_patch MCP tool has two related wire-format bugs that corrupt data when an agent calls it via the MCP stdio transport: (1) top-level array values in patch get wrapped as {item: [...]} when the patch object contains other top-level fields alongside the array, and (2) coerceParamsToSchema does not recurse into ZodObject schemas with .shape missing (the passthrough case used by patch: z.object({}).passthrough()). The cross-reference-fields plan worked around (1) by patching the array alone, then scalars alone. Plan 260609-adopt-instruction-layer hit both bugs in Phase 3 and required an operator-approved node -e escape hatch to unwrap the corrupted value. Proposed fix: (a) extend coerceParamsToSchema to recurse into passthrough ZodObjects AND unwrap {item: [...]} when the target field is a ZodArray; (b) add a regression test file that calls meta_state_patch over the stdio transport with a combined patch (array + scalars) and asserts the stored value is a flat array, not a wrapped object. The structural fix (Bridge 5) is to derive the patch tool schema from the loop-design schema at startup so passthrough goes away. Until then, this design ships the symptom-level fix. Addresses 2 gaps: the meta_state_patch array wrap (newly observed) and the boolean coercion in meta_state_report (related symptom of the same wire-format root cause).

description: Agent stuck in meta_state_refresh_fingerprint loop for 53 minutes (163 calls, 147 returning identical not_grounded error). Direct recurrence of the tool-retry-loop pattern documented in meta-260606T2106Z-agent-called-meta-state-log-change-mcp-tool-5-times-in-succe (subtype tool-retry-loop, status=expired, resolved by auto-resolve). Both findings describe the same compound failure: (1) the agent's thinking channel correctly diagnoses the loop, the action channel retried anyway (model reflex pathology); (2) the target tool lacks a server-side idempotency / dedup guard, so the retry storm is mechanically possible. The earlier finding was resolved by auto-resolve without a code fix; the gap is therefore still open and reproducing. Empirical trace from session dac9b6ed-b6f7-470b-b09b-ed744fbbdff5: agent called meta_state_refresh_fingerprint 153 times on a single id (meta-260610T1504Z-...) that had mechanism_check=null. The tool returned {error: not_grounded, reason: 'mechanism_check is not true; nothing to refresh'} every time. The agent's own thinking channel produced 115 verbatim copies of 'I need to stop making the same redundant call. Let me verify the backfill using relationships instead.' plus 4 close variants, then called refresh again. The user had to cancel the session manually after two operator interventions failed to break the reflex. The fix needs to be mechanical, not prompt-level: (a) auto-default mechanism_check=true when evidence_code_ref is provided in meta_state_report (closes the confusing-error half), (b) idempotency cache on meta_state_refresh_fingerprint keyed on (id, previous_code_fingerprint) with 60s TTL (caps the blast radius of any model reflex). Design captured in loop-design-meta-state-refresh-fingerprint-circuit-breaker; brainstorm at plans/reports/brainstorm-260610-1900-meta-state-refresh-loop-circuit-breaker.md.
resolution: Shipped idempotency cache on meta_state_refresh_fingerprint (60s TTL) and auto-default mechanism_check:true on meta_state_report. Plan 260610-1900-meta-state-refresh-loop-circuit-breaker.

title: Meta-state refresh-fingerprint loop circuit-breaker (idempotency cache + report auto-default)
description: Two-phase fix for the meta_state_refresh_fingerprint retry-loop pathology. Phase 1: in-handler idempotency cache (Map keyed on (id, previous_code_fingerprint), 60s TTL) collapses repeat calls within the same MCP server process to a single result, with a cache_hit: true flag for observability. Phase 2: auto-default mechanism_check: true when evidence_code_ref is present in meta_state_report (using ?? not ||, so explicit mechanism_check: false is preserved), plus a structured warnings array in the response when the caller passes evidence_code_ref with explicit mechanism_check: false. Both phases together: the auto-default closes the confusing-error half (a finding with evidence_code_ref and mechanism_check: null is unreachable), and the cache caps the blast radius of any model reflex that survives (153 identical calls collapse to 1). Compound fix is required: shipping only the cache leaves a UX bug (silent miss when agents provide evidence_code_ref without mechanism_check); shipping only the auto-default leaves a structural gap (any model reflex that re-runs refresh 100+ times still wastes 53 minutes). Touchpoints: meta-state-refresh-fingerprint-tool.js (~10 lines for the cache + get/set + wire-up + export _clearCacheForTests) and meta-state-report-tool.js (~9 lines: change one spread, add 8 lines for warnings). Tests: 4 cache tests (same key returns cache_hit, different fingerprint is miss, TTL expiry re-runs, error responses also cached) + 5 report tests (default-true when evidence_code_ref provided, no warning on explicit true, warning on explicit false, no field when both absent, escape hatch when only mechanism_check provided). Plan path: plans/260610-1900-meta-state-refresh-loop-circuit-breaker/. Brainstorm: plans/reports/brainstorm-260610-1900-meta-state-refresh-loop-circuit-breaker.md. Predecessor finding meta-260606T2106Z-agent-called-meta-state-log-change-mcp-tool-5-times-in-succe resolved by auto-resolve without code fix, leaving the gap open.

Top-level array values under the passthrough ZodObject patch field get wrapped as {item: [...]} by the MCP SDK transport, and the coerceParamsToSchema recursion in tool-registry.js does not unwrap them because metaStateEntryPatchSchema = z.object({}).passthrough() has no inner shape, so the inner field's typeName is never resolved. Same wrap class as meta-260610T1504Z-... deny-list gap and meta-260610T1458Z-... wire-format coercion fix that shipped for meta_state_propose_design + meta_state_report but not meta_state_patch#proposed_design_for (the patch tool's passthrough schema is the structural blocker — typeName is null after unwrap so the inner field never enters the unwrapItemWrap branch). Canonical fix is Bridge 5 (loop-design-schema-as-source-of-truth-bridge-5-derive-tool-schemas-from) — derive the patch tool schema from metaStateEntrySchema at startup so the passthrough goes away and the existing unwrapItemWrap helper unwraps array fields automatically. Until then, two workarounds: (a) tolerate both shapes in every reader (current state, applied ad-hoc during the 260611-1700 plan — 4 readers updated: buildRegistrySummary, fix-loop-design-refs.mjs, cold-tier-regression.test.js, fix-loop-design-refs.test.js); (b) split the patch into array-only + scalar-only calls (the cross-reference-fields plan's pre-260609 workaround, requires two MCP round-trips). Estimated cost: a Bridge 5 plan would be ~6h (derive patch tool schema from metaStateEntrySchema, add patch-only fields, regression test, fingerprint refresh across the 4 affected reader paths). Cold-tier query: meta_state_list({ entry_kind: 'finding', subtype: 'next-up-design', status: 'active' }) should return this alongside the other 2 next-ups (meta-260611T1300Z-..., meta-260610T1504Z-...) as Bridge 5 work. Subtype: next-up-design (matches the convention used by meta-260609T2102Z-next-up-adopt-loop-design-instruction-layer-..., meta-260609T2102Z-next-up-adopt-loop-design-cross-reference-fields-..., meta-260610T1458Z-..., meta-260610T1504Z-..., meta-260611T1253Z-...). The 4 ad-hoc reader patches shipped with plan 260611-1700 are a temporary fix; the next plan adopting this finding should re-derive the patch schema at startup and revert the 4 reader patches.
resolution: Wire-format quirk eliminated at the structural level. `meta_state_patch#patch` is now strict-typed (per-kind union via `buildPatchSchemaFor`), so top-level arrays round-trip flat without the `{item: [...]}` envelope. Wrapped `{item: [...]}` inputs are now REJECTED at the Zod boundary (verified: `coerceParamsToSchema` does NOT recurse into `z.union` fields — `tool-registry.js:80` checks `schema.shape`; union has none). The 9 ad-hoc reader-patch sites that tolerated both shapes are reverted; data is flat-only. Registry `unwrapItemWrap` helper at `tool-registry.js:58-75` is preserved (tool-side coercion). Verified by 3 stdio regression tests in `__tests__/meta-state-patch-derived-schema.test.js`.

description: Cold-session L2 probe test is flaky due to fixed 60s timeout on real droid exec process. The test at cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs:169 ("agent cites code via meta_state_report and local:meta-state refs") spawns a real droid exec --auto medium process and expects 2 sequential MCP calls (meta_state_report + meta_state_patch) to complete within 60s. Agent latency varies run-to-run; the test passes when the agent is fast enough and fails when it is not. Debugging session 2026-06-13: baseline passed 8/8, but adding 1 discoverability hint (~400 chars) to the hook mirror pushed the agent past the timeout boundary on 3 consecutive runs (62-78s vs 60s budget). The droid process exited 0 in all cases (it completed, just slowly). Root cause: the test has no mechanism to detect partial completion or retry. The agent makes 1 of 2 required calls and the assertion (findings.length >= 2) fails. The test cannot distinguish "agent decided not to call" from "agent ran out of time". Fix approaches: (1) poll meta-state.jsonl for the expected entries instead of relying on process exit, (2) increase timeout to 90s with a warning log when >60s, (3) split into 2 separate tests each requiring only 1 MCP call, (4) use a result-based async mechanism if droid exec supports it.
resolution: Fixed two root causes: (1) assertion expected 2 separate finding entries but meta_state_patch updates in-place (1 entry), (2) 60s timeout too tight for agent latency. Changes: timeout 60s→90s, assertion checks both calls landed on same entry via evidence_code_ref + evidence_journal field presence, added polling diagnostics for partial completion visibility.

title: Session-closeout audit agent (replaces ck:journal)
description: Session-closeout audit agent: before closing a session, an automated agent scans the session transcript + filesystem deltas + tool-call history for any problems that occurred but were not yet filed to meta-state (finding, change-log, or rule). For each unfiled problem, the agent auto-files the appropriate entry kind. Replaces the manual `ck:journal` mechanism (the operator-or-agent writes a journal entry at session end) with a loop-owned enforcement surface that cannot be forgotten. The motivation is the recurring pattern observed across 2026-06-06..2026-06-13: agents (including this one) ship code changes, gate fixes, and tool deletions without filing findings for the bugs they encountered en route; the next session has to rediscover the same problems (4 pre-existing test failures filed 2026-06-13, all of which had been latent for 2+ days; the L2 probe test flakiness pattern recurring 2026-06-10 and again 2026-06-13; the context-pollution-stale-workaround-language pattern where a fixed bug's workaround language persisted in AGENTS.md because the agent applying the fix did not file a follow-up). The design space: (1) hook-based: SessionEnd hook scans the transcript + gate-decision log for unfiled problems, prompts the agent before close, (2) MCP-tool-based: ck:closeout MCP tool that the agent calls before session end, (3) workflow-based: workflow_session_closeout triggered by the orchestrator, (4) ad-hoc convention: the agent is supposed to call meta_state_report on any unfiled problem (current state, often skipped). The recommended path is (1) hook-based: it is the only path that cannot be skipped, and it is the path the loop already uses for SessionStart (loop-surface-inject.cjs, H1-H14 hint injection). The hook would: (a) collect all gate-decision blocks (decision: block, decision: escalate) emitted during the session; (b) collect all TODO/HACK/FIXME/XXX comments added to the diff; (c) collect all untracked files at session end; (d) for each, check meta_state_list for an existing finding/change-log with the same evidence_code_ref; (e) if no match, file a finding with the session as session_id and the gate decision / TODO comment / untracked file as evidence. The agent is then asked to ack-or-amend the auto-filed findings before session close. This unifies the audit trail across all session activities: code changes ship with the change-log (already enforced), but the *problems* the agent encountered en route (gate blocks, TODOs left in code, untracked files that look like scratch work) get captured too. description: Import-chain analysis is the canonical dead-code detection method; keyword-based search misses transitive core dependencies. The anti-pattern has recurred 4 times across dead-code cleanup plans: (1) plan 260613-1000 missed core/candidate-to-experiment/{experiment-draft-builder,template-registry}.js + __tests__/bridge-2-unit.test.js; (2) plan 260613-1421 caught (keyword scan worked for that batch); (3) plan 260613-1530 missed core/{schema-description-loader,schema-loader,yaml-parse-wrapper}.js + scout/index.js; (4) this session caught all 7 via grep + reverse import map. Total: 11 stranded files across 2 of 4 cleanup passes. Process fix: when a tool file is deleted, run import-chain analysis on its dependencies (walk all .js files, extract import/require statements, build reverse map, find files with zero live importers excluding entry points) before declaring the cleanup complete. Keyword matching (substring search for the deleted file's basename) is deprecated for this task. The 'meta-260613T1546Z-dead-code-cleanup-process-is-keyword-based-not-import-chain' finding (now resolved) captured the same lesson; this finding elevates it to a meta-pattern. Promote to rule entry so the loop enforces the import-chain step before any tool deletion.

Gate-enforced rule: rule-import-chain-analysis-after-tool-deletion. Pattern type=regex; pattern=rm\s+[^\s]*-tool\.js|rm\s+.*tools/learning-loop-mcp/[^\s]+\.js|git\s+rm\s+[^\s]*-tool\.js. Agent-side consult: when the agent is about to delete a tool file or any .js file under tools/learning-loop-mcp/, the loop reminds the agent to run import-chain analysis (walk all .js files, extract import/require statements, build reverse map, find files with zero live importers) before declaring the cleanup complete. Keyword matching (substring search for the deleted file's basename) is deprecated for this task. Originates from finding meta-260613T1615Z which captured the 4-occurrence meta-pattern (11 stranded files across 2 of 4 cleanup plans 260613-1000, 260613-1421, 260613-1530, this session). description: Cold-session discoverability test (cold-session-discoverability.test.cjs) is flaky and hard to debug. 21 meta-state entries with session_id=test-cold-session-mcp-client-loading exist (8 archived, 8 superseded, 3 resolved, 1 active, 1 reported). description: No MCP path exists to unarchive a meta-state entry or transition out of `status: archived`. The IMMUTABLE_PATCH_FIELDS deny-list in meta-state-patch-tool.js:6-23 blocks any attempt to clear `archived_at` / `archived_by` / `archived_reason` via meta_state_patch. In session 06085a38 (transcript line 465) the recovery from an erroneous rule archival required a direct Python edit of meta-state.jsonl, which bypassed the gate/audit surface, skipped the CAS path through updateEntry, and left `resolved_at`/`resolution` fields on the now-active entry. P0/P1 fixes for the same incident shipped (archive/resolve entry-kind guards, session_id discoverability, bulk-archive preview). The recommended remediation per plans/reports/debugger-260614-0207-session-06085a38-meta-state-process-gaps.md is either (a) extend meta_state_patch to allow archived -> active transitions and auto-clear archived_* fields, or (b) add a dedicated meta_state_unarchive tool. Direct JSONL edits must not remain a recovery path.

No automated registry-consistency check exists to detect entries whose `status` and audit fields disagree. After the manual restore in session 06085a38, meta-state.jsonl:19 carried `status: active` together with `resolved_at` + `resolution` (Finding 4 of plans/reports/debugger-260614-0207-session-06085a38-meta-state-process-gaps.md). The P0 cleanup removed the visible residue, but a defense-in-depth probe is needed so the next drift is caught at the registry layer, not by an operator. Expected invariants: `status: active` must not carry `resolved_at` / `resolution`; `status: archived` must carry `archived_at` / `archived_by` / `archived_reason`; `status: resolved` must carry `resolved_by`; `status: superseded` must carry `consolidated_into`. Remediation: add a test under tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/ that walks the live registry and fails on any status/audit-field mismatch, OR expose a `meta_state_consistency_check` MCP probe that returns the list of drifted entries without mutating state.

description:
Two related gaps in the bash gate (tools/learning-loop-mcp/hooks/bash-gate.js + core/gate-logic.js#applyPromotedRules):

1. False positive on content inside `node -e "..."` string literals. Empirical probes 2026-06-14 confirmed: `node -e "console.log('do not create a new schema')"` and `node -e "console.log('propose a new artifact')"` both escalate with rule-no-new-artifact-types, even though the trigger phrase is inside a JavaScript string literal. The quote-aware splitSegments (P2 from meta-260605T2010Z-...) + stripMessageFlags were designed to keep quoted message bodies whole so the regex sees only the command verb (e.g. 'git commit' from 'git commit -m "..."'). They work for known message flags (-m, --message) but NOT for `node -e` (or `python -c`, `ruby -e`, `perl -e`, `bash -c`, etc.). The regex still matches the literal text inside the quoted body.

2. Opaque error to the agent. When the bash gate blocks a tool call, the agent receives the generic 'Error: Tool execution blocked by hook' message from the Claude Code / Droid CLI runtime. The gate's actual JSON decision (decision, reason, rule_id, matched_pattern) goes to stdout and is captured by the tool runtime, not surfaced back to the agent. Combined with gap #1, this means an agent invoking a benign script can be silently blocked with no actionable feedback, and the only way to diagnose is to manually reproduce via spawnSync.

Reproduction: 11 probes via /tmp/probe-bash-gate-2.mjs 2026-06-14 — P1, P2, P3, P4, P5, P6, P7, P8, P9, P11, P12, P13, P14, P15, P16, P20 all return exit 0; P10, P17, P18, P19 correctly escalate/block. The asymmetry: the gate correctly handles the simple cases, but the `node -e` body and any content with trigger phrases inside a quoted string literal gets over-blocked.

Fixes to consider (separate plans):
- Extend stripMessageFlags to recognize `node -e`, `node --eval`, `python -c`, `python3 -c`, `ruby -e`, `perl -e`, `bash -c`, `sh -c` as message-flag-like. Pass the post-strip body to applyPromotedRules instead of the full command.
- Surface the gate's decision JSON back to the agent in a structured form (e.g., add it to the error message, or write it to a tmp file the agent can read).

Not a security regression — the rule is still enforcing correctly when the trigger is real. Just a usability and false-positive gap that wastes agent cycles.
resolution: Both gaps closed: gap #1 (node -e body false positive) closed by Step 3 ship (change-log meta-260615T1921Z — stripNodeEvalBody in tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/gate-logic.js, with bypass risk documented in finding meta-260615T1920Z); gap #2 (opaque error to agent / decision visibility) closed by Step 2 ship (change-log meta-260615T1459Z — hookSpecificOutput + .gate-decision.log + gate_override tool). The asymmetric design (only node -e, not python -c/bash -c) is locked in by regression-guard tests in __tests__/gate-logic-quoted-strings.test.js. Bypass risk for node -e is caught by Step 2's gate_check_recurrence if it recurs N>=3 in M<=10min. Parent finding closable.

description:
The runtime-agnostic pattern is real in this codebase (shim-not-fork, protocol-adapter, cross-surface iteration) but is NOT codified as a rule. The pattern exists in the code; what's missing is the codified invariant + the surfaces that teach it to future features and audit compliance.

Two existing asymmetries surface the gap:

1. tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/gate-logic.js:407 — GLOB_SCOPE_WHITELIST = ["product/", "docs/", "plans/", "tools/", ".factory/", "meta-state.jsonl"]. The list includes ".factory/" but NOT ".claude/". A future Claude-targeted glob pattern (e.g., a Claude-coordination equivalent of rule-project-skill-boundary) would be silently dropped by the whitelist check. This is a known asymmetry between the two supported runtimes.

2. tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/inbound-state.js:34-65 — readLastOperatorMessage hard-codes both .claude/coordination/ and .factory/coordination/ paths inline. The cross-surface iteration is duplicated logic; if a third surface is added, the function needs editing in two places (lines 45-54 + 59-69). Same anti-pattern recurs in the bash gate's PATH_WRITE_PATTERNS which lists ".claude/coordination/" and ".factory/coordination/" markers separately (tools/learning-loop-mcp/hooks/bash-gate.js:31-41).

The fix is the layered design captured in plans/reports/brainstorm-260615-1400-runtime-agnostic-features-rule.md. The design authors 6 implementation artifacts:

- AGENTS.md §2 amendment (the design spec; subsection "Runtime-Agnostic Pattern" + 6-item checklist)
- Meta-state rule entry rule-runtime-agnostic-features (the codified invariant; enforcement: agent, pattern_type: consult-checklist, pattern = JSON-serialized 6-item checklist)
- New pattern type consult-checklist in core/gate-logic.js#applyPromotedRules (no-op for command-time; recognized for the rule to load; precedent: resolution-evidence-required added 2026-06-06 in meta-260606T1656Z-...)
- New MCP tool check_runtime_agnostic at tools/learning-loop-mcp/tools/check-runtime-agnostic-tool.js (the audit surface; agent-consulted; new agent-manifest group runtime_agnostic)
- core/surfaces.js helper (the cross-surface API; SURFACES = [".claude", ".factory"] is the single source of truth; helpers getAllCoordinationPaths / writeToAllSurfaces / readFromAllSurfaces)
- __tests__/runtime-agnostic.test.js (the regression guard; locks in the existing universal pattern; catches new violations at test time)

Plus 2 refactors that fix the existing asymmetries:
- core/gate-logic.js#GLOB_SCOPE_WHITELIST — refactor to use surfaces.js#SURFACES; closes the .claude/ asymmetry.
- core/inbound-state.js#readLastOperatorMessage — refactor to use surfaces.js#readFromAllSurfaces; DRY the cross-surface iteration.

The 6-item checklist (the rule's pattern):
1. core-in-universal-location — core logic in tools/learning-loop-mcp/{core,hooks,tools}/, not under .claude/ or .factory/
2. shims-in-sync — if hooks are needed, both .claude/coordination/hooks/ and .factory/coordination/hooks/ have a shim that delegates to the universal hook
3. protocol-adapter-i-o — hook stdin/stdout goes through protocol-adapter.js (parseInput, formatOutput, normalizeToolName)
4. manifest-registered — new MCP tools registered in tools/learning-loop-mcp/agent-manifest.json with group and description
5. cross-surface-iteration — code touching surface-specific paths uses the surfaces.js helper, not hard-coded .claude/ or .factory/ paths
6. parameterized-for-new-surfaces — SURFACES is the single source of truth; adding a new runtime is a 1-line append, not a refactor

Resolution criteria (all 6 must hold):
- AGENTS.md §2 has the "Runtime-Agnostic Pattern" subsection listing the 6 items
- meta-state.jsonl has rule-runtime-agnostic-features active with enforcement: agent, pattern_type: consult-checklist
- core/gate-logic.js#applyPromotedRules handles consult-checklist as a no-op (with optional debug warning)
- tools/learning-loop-mcp/tools/check-runtime-agnostic-tool.js exists, registered in agent-manifest.json runtime_agnostic group, returns structured feedback with fix_suggestion
- tools/learning-loop-mcp/core/surfaces.js exists; SURFACES is the only place runtime names are listed; helpers are tested
- tools/learning-loop-mcp/__tests__/runtime-agnostic.test.js passes (regression guard; mutation test: adding a hard-coded surface path to core/ fails the test)
- core/gate-logic.js#GLOB_SCOPE_WHITELIST uses surfaces.js#SURFACES (closes the .claude/ asymmetry)
- core/inbound-state.js#readLastOperatorMessage uses surfaces.js#readFromAllSurfaces (DRY)
- All 840+ existing tests pass; 0 regressions; new tests for all 6 artifacts

The design honors AGENTS.md §6 (Internalization Rule: cite the code, not the markdown). It is a meta-surface rule per §1: the only bound surface; the product surface is unbound and re-debated from the meta-surface. The 6-item checklist is a design-time invariant for the loop itself — the loop's self-model includes "features must be runtime-agnostic". The rule surfaces in loop_describe warm tier via the discoverability_hints mechanism. Future runtimes add themselves by appending to SURFACES; the loop doesn't auto-detect runtimes.

description: inbound-gate.js still contains a local TTL-based staleness check (`findStaleObservations`, lines 50-57) that duplicates the semantics of `core/inbound-state.js#checkObservationStaleness`. The old plan 260614-1856-GH-1259-fix-stale-records-references phase 2 step 5 asked to consolidate them, but that plan is completed and the step was never executed. A direct consolidation is blocked by the F1 fix from plan 260517-2300-gate-v2-staleness-fixes: the inbound gate must only write the operator-message marker when observations are actually stale, because `checkObservationStaleness` requires an existing marker to detect staleness. Removing `findStaleObservations` and calling `checkObservationStaleness` would either violate F1 (marker written unconditionally) or prevent the gate from ever bootstrapping (no marker → stale:false forever). Needs a new design/session independent of plan 260614-1856: either a unified staleness primitive that supports both TTL bootstrap and marker-relative semantics, or a change to the F1 invariant. Replaces/supersedes the resolved finding meta-260614T2138Z-plan-260614-1856-gh-1259-fix-stale-records-references-phase, which was tied to the old plan.
